title:
Lesson 1:
Making plans
description: Using near future to make plans: 'vamos a' (we're going to), invitations, accepting/declining.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Voy a ir al cine. ¿Quieres venir?
dialogue.english: I'm going to the movies. Do you want to come?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: We're going to go on Saturday.

title:
Lesson 2:
Asking permission
description: '¿Puedo...?' = Can I...? / May I...? Polite way to ask for permission.

title:
Lesson 3:
Telephone phrases
description: Phone vocabulary: llamar (to call), teléfono (phone), mensaje (message), hablar (to speak).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No, she's not here. Who's speaking?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you want to leave a message?

title:
Lesson 4:
Expressing preferences
description: 'Preferir' (to prefer): 'prefiero' = I prefer. Irregular (e→ie). Use with nouns or infinitives.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you prefer coffee or tea?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Do you prefer to read or watch television?

title:
Lesson 5:
Asking for opinions
description: Opinion phrases: '¿Qué piensas?' (What do you think?), '¿Qué opinas?' (What's your opinion?), 'Creo que...' (I think that...).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you think of this movie?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What's your opinion of the restaurant?

title:
Lesson 6:
Giving suggestions
description: 'Deber' (should): 'debes' = you should. 'Deberías' = you should (conditional). Giving advice.

title:
Lesson 7:
Expressing agreement
description: Agreement: 'Estoy de acuerdo' (I agree), 'Tienes razón' (You're right), 'Exacto' (Exactly), 'Claro' (Of course).

title:
Lesson 8:
Expressing disagreement
description: Disagreement: 'No estoy de acuerdo' (I don't agree), 'No creo' (I don't think so), 'Al contrario' (On the contrary).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, al contrario, es muy emocionante.
dialogue.english: Yes, on the contrary, it's very exciting.

title:
Lesson 9:
Making invitations
description: Inviting someone: '¿Quieres...?' (Do you want to...?), '¿Te gustaría...?' (Would you like to...?).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you want to go to the concert?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I would like to go.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: When do you want to go?

title:
Lesson 10:
Accepting invitations
description: Accepting: '¡Claro!' (Sure!), '¡Por supuesto!' (Of course!), 'Me encantaría' (I would love to), '¡Perfecto!' (Perfect!).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you want to come to my house?

title:
Lesson 11:
Declining invitations
description: Declining politely: 'Lo siento' (I'm sorry), 'No puedo' (I can't), 'Tal vez otro día' (Maybe another day).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you want to go to the movies tonight?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Tengo que estudiar. Tal vez otro día.
dialogue.english: I have to study. Maybe another day.

title:
Lesson 12:
Reactions & What Happened
description: '¿En serio?' = Really? / Seriously? '¿Qué pasó?' = What happened? '¿Estás bromeando?' = Are you joking? These are common reactions when hearing surprising news.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Listen, they offered me the job.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Sí, pero también hubo un problema.
dialogue.english: Yes, but there was also a problem.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: El sueldo es la mitad de lo que esperaba.
dialogue.english: The salary is half of what I expected.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Are you joking? That can't be!

title:
Lesson 13:
What Are You Doing / Saying
description: '¿Qué haces?' = What do you do? / What are you doing? '¿Qué estás haciendo?' = What are you doing right now? (more emphatic). '¿Qué dijiste?' = What did you say?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Dijiste algo raro hace un momento.
dialogue.english: You said something weird a moment ago.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: What did you say I said?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you normally do on Sundays?

vocabulary.english: what are you doing? / what do you do?

title:
Lesson 14:
Which / How Are You / Since When
description: '¿Cuál?' = Which one? '¿Cuál te gusta?' = Which one do you like? '¿Cómo estás?' = How are you? '¿Cómo has estado?' = How have you been? '¿Desde cuándo?' = Since when?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Good, thanks. And you? How have you been?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Muy bien. Tengo un carro nuevo.
dialogue.english: Very well. I have a new car.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Since when do you have a car?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Desde el mes pasado. ¿Cuál prefieres, rojo o azul?
dialogue.english: Since last month. Which do you prefer, red or blue?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Which one do you like more?
